chats.text: Before we dive into our March roadmap, I did want to surface that February closed just below our projected performance forecast models. right?
Hey, remember the golf trip we had planned for next week? Yeah. I think you forgot.
Right, so— let me reiterate that our February attainment shortfall is primarily a function of team time prevalence delta.
I literally just clocked out. The golf agenda is already set for next Tuesday.
And I think go ahead— getting that here is that we effectively were affected by February shrunk in chronological architecture, right?
Did you see the meeting invite for next quarter? They made me chair it. I have no idea what that means.
Absolutely.
I'm going to be late. The traffic models are projecting a significant delay.
And to take a different angle here— does the under-convergence of the February benchmark offer a strategic acceleration opportunity for March?
I saw that new project management software. The one they're all talking about.
Right. I'll make a note of that for our next review cycle, if you don't mind.
I'm going to need a bit of time off. The quarterly sprint cadence is getting tight.

February closed just below our projected performance forecast models, right?

I think you should consider whether there's a strategic acceleration opportunity here, even if the numbers look tight.

Right. And I'm fine with how that looks on paper—let me know when you'd like to review the updated projections together.
I want to make sure we're not creating a precedent where February's variance becomes the new normal for March planning.
